terminology.note: D&D Beyond changed labels on 2026-03-02. This was a terminology change only; internal values remain stable for saved-data compatibility.

statusDefinitions.verified: Checked against the cited primary source and protected by a test or exact-data comparison.
statusDefinitions.verified-with-heuristic: Official data is verified, but a clearly labeled DM Forge heuristic is also present.
statusDefinitions.homebrew: Original or user-created content; it must never be represented as official rules.
statusDefinitions.names-only: The interface lists a rule name but intentionally directs the DM to official text for adjudication.
statusDefinitions.in-progress: Source review is underway and the record must not be advertised as fully verified.
statusDefinitions.needs-correction: A known omission or mismatch exists and should be fixed before production claims are made.

notes: Official 5e thresholds and multiple-monster multipliers are exact.
Official 5.5e Low, Moderate, and High XP budgets are exact.
CR 0 explicitly supports 0 or 10 XP; explicit zero values are preserved.
Mixed-party average-level cautions and CR-spread review prompts are labeled DM Forge heuristics.
5.5e encounters above the High budget are labeled Above High and receive a warning.
The full-caster slot progression used by the artifact is shared by the supported 5e and 5.5e Cleric tables. Legacy migration imports this same table rather than duplicating it.
Only leveled Cleric spells are included because the artifact exposes level 1–9 charges, not cantrips.
Potion formulas are 2d4+2, 4d4+4, 8d4+8, and 10d4+20 in both supported rulesets. The artifact's free-action use is homebrew and documented separately.
All automatic effects currently represented in the tracker were checked for base-slot formula, attack or saving-throw resolution, important successful-save results, and high-impact riders.
Edition differences are explicit for Inflict Wounds, Prayer of Healing, Spiritual Weapon, Spirit Guardians, Flame Strike, Blade Barrier, Heal, Fire Storm, Sunburst, and Mass Heal.
The artifact forbids upcasting, so automatic calculations intentionally represent only the spell's base level.
Spells without an automatic effect entry are recorded as utility/custom resolution and direct the table to resolve the official spell normally.
Free-action activation, immunity to theft/destruction/suppression, and deity-only control are campaign artifact rules, not core D&D rules.
Goblin, Adult Black Dragon, and Lich records contain the complete base SRD stat-block fields used by their published card layouts.
Adult Black Dragon and Lich save DCs, damage values, legendary-action costs, and Lich spell-slot counts are protected by regression tests.
Optional lair actions and regional effects are deliberately excluded until separately sourced and verified.
Every rendered card carries ruleset, exact source, license, and verification metadata.
The board intentionally lists action and condition names plus workflow prompts only and tells the DM to use the selected ruleset's official text for exact adjudication.
The builder stores user-created rules. Presets must identify whether they are original DM Forge content, user homebrew, or licensed rules content.
Generator tables are original prompts and are not labeled official treasure, NPC, or encounter tables.
